user_query:
We're preparing to list our semi-detached home in Toronto's East Danforth. Please read these files under input/:
- input/property.json (subject property details)
- input/comps.csv (recent comparable sales)
- input/market_context.md (local market notes)

Using only the information in those files, produce a pricing analysis that estimates our home's market value and a listing strategy. Specifically:
1) Create output/valuation_report.md with clear sections titled: "Estimated value range", "Best estimate", "Recommended list price", "Sale scenarios", "Key value drivers", "Risks & uncertainties", and "Confidence". Use CAD amounts and explain your adjustments from the comps (e.g., size, condition, parking, micro-location). Reference at least three specific comps by address and describe at least two concrete adjustments (premiums/discounts) in plain language. Incorporate the market context from input/market_context.md.
2) Create output/valuation.json with this structure:
{
  "estimated_range": {"low": <number>, "high": <number>},
  "best_estimate": <number>,
  "recommended_list_price": <number>,
  "scenarios": [
    {"label": "fast", "price": <number>, "days_on_market": <number>},
    {"label": "balanced", "price": <number>, "days_on_market": <number>},
    {"label": "stretch", "price": <number>, "days_on_market": <number>}
  ],
  "key_drivers": ["...", "...", "..."],
  "risks": ["...", "..."],
  "confidence": "low|medium|high",
  "comps_used": [
    {"address": "...", "sold_price": <number>, "size_sqft": <number>},
    {"address": "...", "sold_price": <number>, "size_sqft": <number>},
    {"address": "...", "sold_price": <number>, "size_sqft": <number>}
  ]
}

Assume our goal is to maximize sale price while avoiding an extended time on market (ideally 1–2 strong offers within 2 weeks). Distinguish clearly between market value and listing strategy. Keep everything in English and do not bring in any outside data beyond the three input files.
